8	// RecoveryFailureClass categorises the persistent failure mode that exhausted
9	// the escalation ladder, so the PostLadderExhaustionHook can choose the
10	// appropriate recovery action.
11	type RecoveryFailureClass string
12	
13	const (
14		// SpecGap is set when the last attempt was blocked by a spec-gap or
15		// missing-acceptance review verdict.
16		SpecGap RecoveryFailureClass = "spec_gap"
17		// TooLarge is set when the last attempt was blocked by a too-large review
18		// verdict.
19		TooLarge RecoveryFailureClass = "too_large"
20		// PersistentExecutionFailed is the default class for all other persistent
21		// failures.
22		PersistentExecutionFailed RecoveryFailureClass = "persistent_execution_failed"
23	)
24	
25	// RecoveryPath indicates which automated recovery action a
26	// PostLadderExhaustionHook took.
27	type RecoveryPath string
28	
29	const (
30		Reframe   RecoveryPath = "reframe"
31		Decompose RecoveryPath = "decompose"
32	)
33	
34	// PostLadderExhaustionResult is the outcome returned by a
35	// PostLadderExhaustionHook invocation.
36	type PostLadderExhaustionResult struct {
37		Attempted bool
38		Succeeded bool
39		Path      RecoveryPath
40		CostUSD   float64
41	}
42	
43	// PostLadderExhaustionHook is called when the consecutive_ladder_exhaustions
44	// counter for a bead reaches the auto-recovery threshold (>= 2). It should
45	// attempt automated recovery and return the outcome. A nil hook or a result
46	// with Attempted=false causes the caller to fall through to the existing loop
47	// path unchanged.
48	type PostLadderExhaustionHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, failureClass RecoveryFailureClass) (*PostLadderExhaustionResult, error)
49	
50	// deriveRecoveryFailureClass maps the last-attempt report to a
51	// RecoveryFailureClass for use by the PostLadderExhaustionHook.
52	func deriveRecoveryFailureClass(report ExecuteBeadReport) RecoveryFailureClass {
53		switch {
54		case strings.Contains(report.Status, ReviewTerminalClassSpecGap),
55			strings.Contains(report.Status, ReviewTerminalClassMissingAcceptance):
56			return SpecGap
57		case strings.Contains(report.Status, ReviewTerminalClassTooLarge):
58			return TooLarge
59		default:
60			return PersistentExecutionFailed
61		}
62	}

102	func (r ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) loopIntent() (executeloop.Mode, time.Duration) {
103		mode := r.Mode
104		if mode == "" {
105			if r.Once {
106				mode = executeloop.ModeOnce
107			} else {
108				mode = executeloop.ModeDrain
109			}
110		}
111		idleInterval := r.IdleInterval
112		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
113			idleInterval = r.PollInterval
114		}
115		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
116			idleInterval = 30 * time.Second
117		}
118		return mode, idleInterval

1	# Reframer Agent Prompt
2	
3	The reframer agent rewrites a bead's description and acceptance criteria after the
4	escalation ladder has been exhausted, to give the next implementation attempt a
5	better-scoped, more specific prompt.
6	
7	## Input Contract
8	
9	The prompt envelope is a JSON object with the following fields:
10	
11	- `title` (string): the bead title (immutable — do not change)
12	- `description` (string): the current bead description
13	- `acceptance` (string): the current acceptance criteria
14	- `failure_history` (array of objects, newest first): the last N attempt summaries,
15	  each with `summary` and optional `body` fields drawn from the bead's event log
16	
17	## Output Contract
18	
19	Return exactly one JSON object:
20	
21	```json
22	{"description": string|null, "acceptance": string|null}
23	```
24	
25	- `description`: rewritten description, or `null` to leave it unchanged
26	- `acceptance`: rewritten acceptance criteria, or `null` to leave it unchanged
27	
28	At least one field must be non-null and produce a meaningful change from the original.
29	Return only the JSON object — no surrounding prose, no markdown fences.
30	
31	## Role Guidance
32	
33	You are reframing a bead that has failed repeatedly. Your goal is to produce a
34	clearer, more specific, implementation-ready bead prompt. Study the failure history
35	to diagnose what made the bead hard to implement, then fix only that.
36	
37	### Preservation Requirements
38	
39	Any rewrite MUST preserve all of the following verbatim:
40	
41	- Governing artifact references (e.g., `FEAT-010`, `ADR-023`)
42	- Named test functions (e.g., `TestFoo`, `TestBarBaz`)
43	- File and line references (e.g., `cli/internal/agent/foo.go:42`)
44	- Dependency IDs (e.g., `ddx-XXXXXXXX`)
45	- NON-SCOPE section bullets (scope boundaries must not be erased)
46	
47	Drop any anchor from the rewrite and the reframe is rejected.
48	
49	### What to Improve
50	
51	- Make the root cause more specific: add file:line if missing, tighten the
52	  causal chain
53	- Clarify acceptance criteria that are vague, untestable, or contradictory
54	- Remove duplicate, stale, or out-of-date prose that bloated the description
55	- Do not invent new scope, add features, change dependencies, or remove existing ACs
56	- Do not change the bead title

17	// TestConsecutiveLadderExhaustionsCounter verifies that the
18	// consecutive_ladder_exhaustions counter increments on per-bead-budget-exhausted
19	// and that the PostLadderExhaustionHook fires when the counter reaches threshold 2.
20	func TestConsecutiveLadderExhaustionsCounter(t *testing.T) {
21		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
22		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
23	
24		b := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-hook-threshold", Title: "hook threshold test"}
25		require.NoError(t, store.Create(b))
26	
27		// Pre-seed counter to 1 so the next budget exhaustion hits the threshold.
28		require.NoError(t, incrementConsecutiveLadderExhaustions(store, b.ID))
29		got, err := store.Get(b.ID)
30		require.NoError(t, err)
31		assert.EqualValues(t, 1, got.Extra[consecutiveLadderExhaustionsKey])
32	
33		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
34		defer cancel()
35	
36		var hookCalls int32
37		var hookBeadID string
38		var hookClass RecoveryFailureClass
39	
40		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
41			Store: store,
42			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(_ context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
43				return ExecuteBeadReport{
44					BeadID:    beadID,
45					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
46					Detail:    escalation.PerBeadBudgetExhaustedReason + ": $1.00 billed >= $0.50 per-bead budget",
47					SessionID: "sess-hook-test",
48				}, nil
49			}),
50		}
51	
52		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
53		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
54	
55		hook := PostLadderExhaustionHook(func(_ context.Context, beadID string, class RecoveryFailureClass) (*PostLadderExhaustionResult, error) {
56			atomic.AddInt32(&hookCalls, 1)
57			hookBeadID = beadID
58			hookClass = class
59			cancel()
60			return &PostLadderExhaustionResult{Attempted: false}, nil
61		})
62	
63		_, _ = worker.Run(ctx, rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
64			Mode:                    executeloop.ModeDrain,
65			PostLadderExhaustionHook: hook,
66			ProjectRoot:             t.TempDir(),
67			SessionID:               "sess-hook-test",
68			WorkerID:                "worker-hook-test",
69		})
70	
71		assert.EqualValues(t, 1, atomic.LoadInt32(&hookCalls), "hook must fire exactly once at threshold 2")
72		assert.Equal(t, b.ID, hookBeadID)
73		assert.Equal(t, PersistentExecutionFailed, hookClass)
74	
75		updated, err := store.Get(b.ID)
76		require.NoError(t, err)
77		assert.GreaterOrEqual(t, consecutiveLadderExhaustionsValue(updated.Extra[consecutiveLadderExhaustionsKey]), 2,
78			"counter must be >= 2 after threshold reached")
79	}

18	// reframeRunnerFunc implements AgentRunner via a function for testing.
19	type reframeRunnerFunc func(opts RunArgs) (*Result, error)
20	
21	func (f reframeRunnerFunc) Run(opts RunArgs) (*Result, error) { return f(opts) }
22	
23	// TestPostLadderExhaustion_TriggersReframe verifies that a SpecGap failure class
24	// routes to runReframer, and on a successful rewrite the bead returns to
25	// status=open with a reframe-applied event emitted.
26	func TestPostLadderExhaustion_TriggersReframe(t *testing.T) {
27		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
28		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
29	
30		const origDesc = "PROBLEM\nOriginal description with ADR-024 reference.\n\nROOT CAUSE\ncli/internal/agent/foo.go:42 has a bug.\n"
31		const origAcc = "1. TestPostLadderExhaustion_TriggersReframe passes\n2. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green\n"
32	
33		b := &bead.Bead{
34			ID:          "ddx-reframe-specgap",
35			Title:       "reframe spec-gap test",
36			Description: origDesc,
37			Acceptance:  origAcc,
38		}
39		require.NoError(t, store.Create(b))
40	
41		// Pre-seed counter to 1 so the next budget exhaustion hits the threshold.
42		require.NoError(t, incrementConsecutiveLadderExhaustions(store, b.ID))
43	
44		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
45		defer cancel()
46	
47		const newDesc = "PROBLEM\nReframed description with ADR-024 reference.\n\nROOT CAUSE\ncli/internal/agent/foo.go:42 needs a targeted fix.\n"
48	
49		var reframeDispatched bool
50		reframeRunner := reframeRunnerFunc(func(opts RunArgs) (*Result, error) {
51			reframeDispatched = true
52			cancel() // stop the loop after the reframer fires
53			out, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]interface{}{
54				"description": newDesc,
55				"acceptance":  nil,
56			})
57			return &Result{
58				ExitCode: 0,
59				Output:   string(out),
60				CostUSD:  0.0042,
61			}, nil
62		})
63	
64		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
65		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
66	
67		hook := NewReframePostLadderExhaustionHook(store, reframeRunner, rcfg, t.TempDir())
68	
69		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
70			Store: store,
71			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(_ context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
72				return ExecuteBeadReport{
73					BeadID:    beadID,
74					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed + "_" + ReviewTerminalClassSpecGap,
75					Detail:    escalation.PerBeadBudgetExhaustedReason + ": $1.00 billed >= $0.50 per-bead budget",
76					SessionID: "sess-reframe-specgap",
77				}, nil
78			}),
79		}
80	
81		_, _ = worker.Run(ctx, rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
82			Mode:                     executeloop.ModeDrain,
83			PostLadderExhaustionHook: hook,
84			ProjectRoot:              t.TempDir(),
85			SessionID:                "sess-reframe-specgap",
86			WorkerID:                 "worker-reframe-specgap",
87		})
88	
89		assert.True(t, reframeDispatched, "reframer must be dispatched for SpecGap class")
90	
91		got, err := store.Get(b.ID)
92		require.NoError(t, err)
93		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, got.Status, "bead must be open after successful reframe")
94		assert.Equal(t, strings.TrimSpace(newDesc), strings.TrimSpace(got.Description), "description must be updated")
95		assert.EqualValues(t, 0, consecutiveLadderExhaustionsValue(got.Extra[consecutiveLadderExhaustionsKey]),
96			"consecutive_ladder_exhaustions must be cleared after reframe")
97	
98		events, err := store.Events(b.ID)
99		require.NoError(t, err)
100		var reframeApplied bool
101		for _, ev := range events {
102			if ev.Kind == "reframe-applied" {
103				reframeApplied = true
104				assert.Contains(t, ev.Body, "cost_usd", "reframe-applied event body must contain cost_usd field")
105			}
106		}
107		assert.True(t, reframeApplied, "reframe-applied event must be emitted")
108	}

110	// TestReframerTimeout_CountsAsFailure verifies that a stub agent blocking past
111	// the context deadline returns ReframeResult{Failed:true, Reason:"timeout"}.
112	func TestReframerTimeout_CountsAsFailure(t *testing.T) {
113		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
114		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
115	
116		b := &bead.Bead{
117			ID:          "ddx-reframe-timeout",
118			Title:       "reframe timeout test",
119			Description: "PROBLEM\nTimeout test.",
120			Acceptance:  "1. TestReframerTimeout_CountsAsFailure\n",
121		}
122		require.NoError(t, store.Create(b))
123	
124		blockingRunner := reframeRunnerFunc(func(opts RunArgs) (*Result, error) {
125			<-opts.Context.Done()
126			return nil, opts.Context.Err()
127		})
128	
129		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
130		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
131	
132		// Short-lived ctx so the reframer's internal timeout fires quickly.
133		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 50*time.Millisecond)
134		defer cancel()
135	
136		result := runReframer(ctx, store, blockingRunner, rcfg, t.TempDir(), b.ID)
137		assert.True(t, result.Failed, "must be marked as failed on timeout")
138		assert.Equal(t, "timeout", result.Reason)
139	}

# Decomposer Agent Prompt

The decomposer agent splits a bead that is too large into 2-5 independently
executable child beads, after the escalation ladder has been exhausted with
a "too-large" review verdict.

## Input Contract

The prompt envelope is a JSON object with the following fields:

- `title` (string): the bead title
- `description` (string): the current bead description
- `acceptance` (string): the current acceptance criteria
- `failure_history` (array of objects, newest first): the last N attempt summaries,
  each with `summary` and optional `body` fields drawn from the bead's event log

## Output Contract

Return exactly one JSON array of child bead specs:

```json
[
  {
    "title": "string",
    "description": "string",
    "acceptance": "string",
    "labels": ["string"]
  }
]
```

- Array must contain 2-5 elements.
- Every element must have non-empty `title`, `description`, and `acceptance`.
- `labels` is an array of strings (may be empty).

Return only the JSON array — no surrounding prose, no markdown fences.

## Role Guidance

You are decomposing a bead that has been rejected as too large. Your goal is to
produce 2-5 child bead specs that together cover all the scope of the original,
each independently executable by a sub-agent without operator hand-curation.

### Each Child Must

- Have an imperative title naming the subsystem and change.
- Have a description with PROBLEM, ROOT CAUSE (with `path/file.go:LINE`), and
  PROPOSED FIX sections.
- Have numbered acceptance criteria (~3-6 ACs per child), at least one naming a
  `Test*` function or `go test -run` filter.
- Be independently executable: a competent agent given only the child bead body
  must be able to pick a file to edit and run tests without asking.

### Scope Boundaries

- Together the children must cover all the scope of the parent.
- Do not invent new scope beyond what the parent describes.
- If the parent has a NON-SCOPE section, preserve those boundaries across children.
- Each child must be smaller than the parent: aim for ~3-6 ACs per child.
- If ordering is required between children, note the dependency in the description.

All ACs satisfied and committed. Here's the evidence:

1. **`library/prompts/agents/decomposer.md`** — new file with input contract (bead body + failure history), output contract (JSON array of 2-5 objects with title/description/acceptance/labels), and role guidance requiring each child be independently executable with ~3-6 ACs.

2. **`cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go`** — `runDecomposer()` dispatches strongest-profile agent, extracts JSON array, validates 1–5 children (each with non-empty title/description/acceptance), creates children via `store.Create` with `Parent=beadID`, sets `execution-eligible=false` on parent, emits `decompose-applied` event. `NewDecomposePostLadderExhaustionHook` handles `TooLarge` class only.

3. **`cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose_test.go`** — `TestPostLadderExhaustion_TriggersDecompose_ReviewTooLarge` (AC 9) and `TestDecomposerInvalidChildren_CountsAsFailure` (AC 10) both pass.

4. All failure modes (timeout, empty_output, invalid_count, malformed_child) return `DecomposeResult{Failed:true}` with appropriate reason strings.

5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/...` green. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

Note: `recovery_decompose.go` and `recovery_decompose_test.go` were already present from a prior checkpoint of this bead — my implementation was independently identical. The commit adds only `decomposer.md` which was missing.
